steps.title: Spot the secure legacy
steps.content: The phrase "will always be remembered" establishes that Chojnowska-Liskiewicz's place in history is permanent—a baseline for comparison.

steps.title: Choose the opposite quality
steps.content: Define: the blank contrasts Nancy Bird-Walton's historical memory with Chojnowska-Liskiewicz's, who "will always be remembered" — a secure, permanent legacy. The blank must describe something weaker or less fixed. Substitute each choice: A) deserving — describes moral worthiness, not the security of historical memory; irrelevant contrast; eliminated. B) conspicuous — means prominent or easily noticed, which would actually match or exceed Chojnowska-Liskiewicz's fame, not contrast with it; eliminated. C) uncertain — means ambiguous or not firmly established; this directly contrasts with "will always be remembered"; fits perfectly. D) enduring — means lasting a long time, which would imply Nancy's legacy outlasts the other, the wrong direction; eliminated. Verdict: C is correct.
